preamble: The emergence of the voiced alveolar affricate /dz/ from an empty sound, or zero, is a phenomenon primarily observed through epenthesis, where /dz/ is inserted to break up certain phonological sequences or to facilitate articulation between morphemes.

examples.note: Epenthesis of /dz/ between a nasal prefix and a vowel-initial verb stem, meaning 'teach me'.
Epenthesis of /dz/ between a nasal prefix and a vowel-initial noun stem, meaning 'heels'.

sources: Morrison, M. E. (2011). A Reference Grammar of Bena. (Doctoral dissertation, Rice University).
